title: TLS, Authentication, Virtual Hosts, Authorization, and Secrets · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for tls, authentication, virtual hosts, authorization, and secrets and requires the smallest defensible messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support.
Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Amqps Identity Verification
steps.instruction:
Order the Amqps Identity Verification RabbitMQ and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Amqps Identity Verification.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Amqps Identity Verification assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Amqps Identity Verification, keep Amqps Identity Verification connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.text: A quiz messaging team handles case 4212 in a canary consumer reading old and new envelope versions. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate verified TLS and vhost access while preserving separate configure, write, and read permissions; separate browser evidence from disposable live-broker proof; then introduce a sequence gap for one partition while another stays healthy. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use TLS with trusted CA, hostname and SNI verification, minimum protocol policy, client identity where required, and tested certificate rotation.
